runSummary.oneSentence: Hepatocellular carcinoma is a primary liver malignancy that often emerges from chronically injured tissue and should be discovered through fair clues.
runSummary.patientExperienceSummary: The patient notices vague but escalating symptoms that only make sense once the detectives investigate the hidden liver lesion.
runSummary.keyMechanism: Malignant hepatocyte growth distorts local architecture inside a chronically damaged liver.
runSummary.timeScale: months to years on top of chronic liver disease

franchiseRules: Mystery first: observation and suspicion should lead before explicit naming.
Diagnosis must be earned through clues, not front-loaded as a title card.
Treatment is the action climax and should resolve the mechanism uncovered in the reveal.
The wrap-up must echo the opener so the franchise loop closes cleanly.
Keep art and lettering separate. Do not place large visible text inside generated image prompts.

characterLocks: Detective Cyto Kine is the felt lead investigator with HUD visor and evidence vial
Deputy Pip is the felt field deputy with micro-scanner and learner questions
anatomyLocks: focal abnormal district contrasts with surrounding tissue
styleLocks: panel composition optimized for later lettering
continuityBible.letteringPolicy: Never request visible dialogue, captions, labels, or teaching copy inside the generated art. Apply all text from the separate lettering layer after image generation.

retryGuidance: If a panel loses anatomy clarity, simplify the composition before weakening the medical objective.
If character continuity drifts, explicitly restate the detective locks and reuse the first approved successful panel as the style reference.
If the model adds visible text, remove all requested text from the art prompt and keep overlays in the lettering instructions only.
If a panel fails repeatedly, keep the clinical objective fixed and tighten only composition, continuity, and anatomy locks.

panels.beatGoal: Reveal a suspicious focal region worth investigating.
panels.medicalObjective: Suggest autonomous abnormal growth without naming the diagnosis.
panels.actionSummary: They hover near a district of cells whose architecture breaks the surrounding pattern.
panels.clueRevealed: A focal abnormal region behaves like a hidden stronghold within the liver.
panels.compositionNotes: Contrast the suspicious region against more orderly surrounding tissue.
acceptanceChecks: no explicit diagnosis label in art
clear contrast between normal and abnormal tissue

claimReferences.claimText: HCC reflects malignant transformation of hepatocytes.
Tumor growth changes local perfusion and tissue architecture.

letteringEntries.speaker: Deputy Pip
letteringEntries.text: That district is rewriting the neighborhood. Follow it, but do not name it yet.
letteringEntries.purpose: Land the first major clue while preserving reveal timing.

openAiImagePrompt.prompt: Create a high-fidelity finished comic-book panel illustration. Scene and background: adjacent to suspicious liver region at tissue scale. Subject and action: They hover near a district of cells whose architecture breaks the surrounding pattern. Key medical details: Suggest autonomous abnormal growth without naming the diagnosis. Story purpose: first major clue. Camera and composition: medium shot with focal push-in, slightly canted, Contrast the suspicious region against more orderly surrounding tissue. Lighting and atmosphere: ominous discovery. Continuity anchors: case tablet hologram; jet pack glow; distorted tissue geometry. Character locks: Detective Cyto Kine is the felt lead investigator with HUD visor and evidence vial; Deputy Pip is the felt field deputy with micro-scanner and learner questions. Anatomy and mechanism locks: liver-inspired tissue geometry; focal abnormal district contrasts with surrounding tissue; avoid generic intestine-like visuals. Style and finish locks: action-packed whimsical comic mystery; high clarity; panel composition optimized for later lettering. Leave clean space for later lettering and do not render any visible text in the image.
openAiImagePrompt.negativePrompt: no speech bubbles, no captions, no labels, no medical chart overlays, no gore, no photoreal pathology slide, no extra detectives, no duplicate limbs, no illegible background text
styleLocks: panel composition optimized for later lettering
characterLocks: Detective Cyto Kine is the felt lead investigator with HUD visor and evidence vial
Deputy Pip is the felt field deputy with micro-scanner and learner questions
anatomyLocks: focal abnormal district contrasts with surrounding tissue
notes: Start with an explicit image-creation instruction and keep the request concrete, skimmable, and visual.
If anatomy becomes ambiguous, restate the medical objective and anatomy locks before changing style.
Do not include speech bubbles, captions, or labels inside the generated art.

openAiPanelExecutionPrompt: Create the final panel image set for workflow run "run.hcc.001" from rendering guide "rgd.hcc.001". Generate exactly 1 finished panel image for this guide section, keep page and panel order stable, use only the supplied panel content for Hepatocellular carcinoma, follow scene and background first, then subject and action, then medical details, then framing, lighting, style, and constraints, and keep all visible text out of the art so lettering can be overlaid separately.
